data.title: XRP traders remain optimistic, eyeing $4 despite recent slump. Predicted odds of reaching $4 and above this year have decreased from 53% to 30%.
data.description: XRP has shown a stagnant performance over the past month with a decrease of 8% over the week and 2.50% over the month, eliminating gains from the July rally that almost pushed it towards record highs of $3.84. Despite this setback, there is still optimism among cryptocurrency enthusiasts about the possibility of XRP hitting new all-time highs by the end of the year. Prediction markets reflect this sentiment, with a noticeable drop in confidence as Polymarket traders now give a 30% chance of XRP surpassing $4, down from 53% a week ago. data.title: Hikvision showcases achievements in ecological protection at 5th World Congress of Biosphere Reserves in Hangzhou, China.
data.description: In Hangzhou, China, the 5th World Congress of Biosphere Reserves (WCBR) began on September 22, 2025, representing its first-ever Asian hosting by UNESCO. This significant event brings together stakeholders, with participation from numerous countries, in discussions on conservation and sustainable development. The Congress focuses on achieving global biodiversity targets and promoting the concept of biosphere reserves.

data.title: Global wearable band market sees 50.2 million devices shipped in Q2 2025, up 13% from last year. Omdia predicts 9% growth in 2026.
data.description: In London, the global wearable band market is experiencing significant growth, as indicated by a record 50.2 million devices shipped in Q2 2025, reflecting a 13% increase compared to the previous year. This surge is attributed to high demand for entry-level basic devices that also offer advanced tracking features. Omdia research forecasts an 8% growth in the market for 2025, with an additional 9% increase projected for 2026. This expansion is primarily driven by the escalating consumer interest in health, fitness, and sports, underscoring the rising popularity of wearable bands.

data.title: S&P 500 drops for second day, Dow falls 150+ points. Fear index remains in 'Greed' zone. New single-family home sales up 20.5%.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index indicated a decrease in market sentiment, remaining in the "Greed" zone. On Wednesday, U.S. stocks saw a decline, with the Dow Jones index dropping by over 150 points. The S&P 500 also experienced a second consecutive decline due to decreased shares of Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Oracle (NYSE: ORCL). In economic news, new single-family home sales in the U.S. surged by 20.5% compared to the previous month, reaching an annualized rate of 800,000 units in August. For more details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
